response.justification: A deeply experienced, versatile engineer with a genuine craftsman profile, but a poor match for this specific role. The position requires 8 years of C++ and sustained large-scale compute infrastructure work; the resume shows only brief C/C++ stints (National Instruments, ~6 months, plus a thesis project), with the closest analog being ~2 years of low-latency DLang systems work at Sociomantic. His last decade is predominantly full-stack TypeScript/Node product engineering, and while he mentors actively through workshops and courses, there is no formal technical-leadership role owning cross-team priorities, deadlines, and platform strategy.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests/second in DLang)
Strong engineering-quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, and author of test frameworks and developer tooling
Consistent mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record (multi-day courses, workshops, code dojos)
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ minimum; primary systems language is DLang, not C++
Recent ~8 years are full-stack web/product and AI tooling work, not cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure
No formal technical-lead experience managing cross-team alignment, priorities, and deliverables at platform scale
